key_points: Apartheid was a system of racial segregation in South Africa where black people were denied equal political and social rights
Nelson Mandela and the African National Congress played a major role in ending apartheid
South Africa adopted a democratic constitution to ensure equality and justice for all citizens
A constitution provides a framework for governance and limits the powers of the government
It establishes the structure of political institutions and their functions
The Constituent Assembly of India was formed in 1946 to prepare the Constitution
Members of the Constituent Assembly were elected by provincial legislative assemblies
The Constitution of India reflects the ideals of the freedom struggle
The Preamble outlines the philosophy and goals of the Constitution
Justice, Liberty, Equality and Fraternity are core democratic values in the Constitution
examples: Example of racial discrimination system: Apartheid in South Africa
Leader who fought against apartheid: Nelson Mandela
Indian Constitution drafting body: Constituent Assembly
Chairman of Drafting Committee: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
Democratic values mentioned in the Preamble: Justice, Liberty, Equality, Fraternity
formulas: Constitution → Set of fundamental rules guiding governance
Democracy → Government elected by the people
Sovereign → Independent authority of the nation
Socialist → Aim to reduce economic inequalities
Secular → State treats all religions equally
exam_tips: Understand how apartheid affected South African society
Remember important leaders like Nelson Mandela and Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
Know the year of formation of the Constituent Assembly (1946)
Focus on the values mentioned in the Preamble
Be able to explain why a constitution is necessary for democracy
